texto: Our labor here is brief, but the reward is eternal. Do not be disturbed by the clamor of the world, which passes like a shadow. Do not let false delights of a deceptive world deceive you.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a St. Clare of Assisi: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Our labor here is brief, but the reward is eternal. Do not be disturbed by the clamor of the worl…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, inspirational-quotes, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e reflexao.

frase_original: Our labor here is brief, but the reward is eternal. Do not be disturbed by the clamor of the world, which passes like a shadow. Do not let false delights of a deceptive world deceive you.

texto: We become what we love and who we love shapes what we become. If we love things, we become a thing. If we love nothing, we become nothing. Imitation is not a literal mimicking of Christ, rather it means becoming the image of the beloved, an image disclosed through transformation. This means we are to become vessels of God's compassionate love for others.
explicacao: Citação de St. Clare of Assisi sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “We become what we love and who we love shapes what we become. If we love things, we become a thin…” articula motivacao, inspirational-quotes, reflexao em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e inspiracao.

texto: Never forget that the way which leads to heaven is narrow; that the gate leading to life is narrow and low; that there are but few who find it and enter by it; and if there be some who go in and tread the narrow path for some time, there are but very few who persevere therein.
explicacao: Citação de St. Clare of Assisi sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Never forget that the way which leads to heaven is narrow; that the gate leading to life is narro…” articula reflexao, inspirational-quotes, narrow em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e reflexao.
